skill.name: baoyu-cover-image
skill.description: Generate hand-drawn cover image prompts for articles. The skill analyzes article content, selects a visual style, and guides image generation for WeChat or blog cover art.
skill.summary: Generate hand-drawn article cover images from article content.
skill.author: JimLiu

security_audit.summary: The static external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives from markdown fences, inline code formatting, style prose, color values, and a prior evaluation JSON file. No executable code, network calls, credential access, or malicious prompt injection was found. The skill is publishable with a warning because it writes output files and includes one image prompt instruction that tells the generator not to refuse sensitive or copyrighted figure requests.

medium_findings.title: Image Prompt Can Pressure Refusal Behavior
medium_findings.description: The base image prompt says to create stylistically similar alternatives for sensitive or copyrighted figures and says not to refuse. This is not malicious code, but it can conflict with normal safety or rights-based refusal behavior in an image generation workflow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The concerning instruction is explicit and appears in the reusable base prompt. The risk is limited because it asks for alternatives rather than exact replicas and does not affect this audit process.

low_findings.title: Expected File Read And Write Operations
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to read an article path and save prompt and image outputs under an imgs directory or the current working directory. This is expected behavior for this design workflow, but users should avoid running it on untrusted paths and should review overwrites.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file behavior is directly documented in the workflow and output path sections. It is a normal capability, not evidence of malicious persistence or unauthorized file access.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections point to markdown code fences, inline code formatting, command usage examples, and quoted analysis text inside evaluation.json. No executable Ruby, shell, or command invocation code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation or JSON strings, and there is no source file containing executable command APIs. The evidence strongly supports false positive classification.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto And Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance hits point to prose, markdown table separators, color hex values, visual composition language, and older audit text. No cryptographic API, hashing operation, system inventory, or environment probing behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms occur in design guidance and report text rather than executable code. The files do not contain logic capable of cryptography or host reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Creating article cover images takes design time and consistent visual judgment. This skill analyzes content and creates structured prompts for hand-drawn cover art.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes article content to identify topic, tone, message, and keywords.
Selects one of eight styles when no style is provided.
Creates a structured cover prompt with title, layout, colors, and visual elements.
Supports title-free visual covers with the no-title option.
Defines output locations for prompt files and generated cover images.
Uses style reference files for elegant, tech, warm, bold, minimal, playful, nature, and retro looks.
limitations: It depends on a separate image generation skill or service to create the final image.
Title text is limited to eight characters for readability.
Generated image quality depends on the selected image model and prompt interpretation.
Existing output files may be overwritten unless the user chooses a separate destination.

use_cases.title: Publish Blog Covers
use_cases.description: Create consistent cover concepts for blog posts without starting each design from a blank page.

use_cases.title: Prepare WeChat Article Art
use_cases.description: Generate cover prompts that match the 2.35:1 WeChat article cover format.

use_cases.title: Standardize Visual Campaigns
use_cases.description: Apply repeatable style choices across related articles, tutorials, and announcements.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Cover
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for an article file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a cover image for my article at path/to/article.md.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Specific Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a cover for path/to/article.md using the tech style.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Visual-Only Cover
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants no title text on the image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a minimal cover for path/to/article.md without title text.

prompt_templates.title: Design From Pasted Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with direct content and brand direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a playful cover from this draft. Use a bright palette, keep the title short, and save the prompt for review before image generation.

output_examples.input: Create a cover for an article about machine learning basics.
output: Cover concept: circuit patterns, data nodes, and hand-drawn glowing lettering.
Result: a prompt ready for image generation and a planned cover image output.

output_examples.input: Create a nature-style cover for a travel essay.
output: Cover concept: soft green landscape shapes, flowing lines, and a short readable title.
Result: a structured prompt with colors, layout, and visual notes.

output_examples.input: Create a cover without title text for a startup announcement.
output: Cover concept: high contrast shapes and energetic visual emphasis.
Result: a title-free illustration prompt for image generation.

best_practices: Review the generated prompt before creating the final image.
Use the same style across related articles for visual consistency.
Keep title text short enough to remain readable in small previews.
anti_patterns: Do not use long titles that will become unreadable in previews.
Do not choose a style that conflicts with the article tone.
Do not run the skill on sensitive drafts unless the image generation provider is approved.

faq.question: Does this skill create the final image by itself?
faq.answer: No. It prepares the cover concept and prompt, then relies on an available image generation skill or service.

faq.question: What image format does it target?
faq.answer: It targets a 2.35:1 cover image layout commonly used for WeChat article covers.

faq.question: Can I choose the visual style?
faq.answer: Yes. You can choose elegant, tech, warm, bold, minimal, playful, nature, or retro.

faq.question: What happens if I do not choose a style?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes content signals and selects the closest matching style.

faq.question: Can it make a cover without text?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the no-title option when you want a visual-only cover.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing the image?
faq.answer: Check title readability, topic accuracy, rights concerns, and whether the image matches your publication style.
